directives.title: Configure Platform Allow Lists
steps: Access the Hermes platform configuration settings.
For Slack, Telegram, Mattermost, Matrix, and DingTalk, define specific allow lists.
Restrict interaction with Hermes to only the channels, rooms, or chats specified in these allow lists.
directives.rationale: This directs an agent to modify specific configuration parameters for multiple messaging platforms to enforce access control, a common and discrete task.

directives.title: Add New Model Provider Plugin
steps: Locate the `model providers plugin directory` within the Hermes installation.
Create a new plugin file or directory structure adhering to the plugin interface within this directory.
Implement the necessary code for the new model provider or route within the newly created plugin.
directives.rationale: This task involves creating a new file/module in a specified location following an expected pattern, which an autonomous agent could perform if it understands the plugin structure.

directives.title: Enable Open Router Response Caching
steps: Access the Hermes configuration related to Open Router integration.
Locate the setting for response caching.
Enable response caching for Open Router routes and models, ensuring it applies only where supported.
directives.target: Hermes Open Router configuration (e.g., `open_router_config.yaml` or API settings)
directives.rationale: This is a direct configuration change to activate a specific feature, a clear action for an autonomous agent.

directives.title: Configure Cron Job for Script-Only Execution (No Agent Cron Mode)
steps: Access the Hermes cron job definition files or configuration interface.
For a specific cron job, set the execution mode to 'script-only' or 'no agent cron mode'.
Ensure the configuration dictates that Hermes remains silent if the script produces no output, and delivers output directly if produced.
directives.target: Hermes cron job configuration (e.g., `cron_jobs.yaml` or a dedicated cron config file)
directives.rationale: This involves setting a specific operational mode for a cron job, a clear and discrete configuration task an agent can perform.

directives.title: Configure Per-Task Max Retries in Kanban
steps: Access the Hermes Kanban system configuration.
Locate the parameter for 'max retries' at a per-task level.
Set the desired maximum number of retries for individual tasks.
directives.target: Hermes Kanban task configuration (e.g., `kanban_settings.yaml` or task definition schema)
directives.rationale: This is a direct configuration of a numerical parameter within the Kanban system, a standard and implementable task for an agent.

directives.title: Configure Cron Job for Script-Only Watchdog Mode
steps: Identify the specific cron job configuration to be modified.
Set the cron job's execution mode to 'script-only watchdog' (or equivalent setting) within its configuration.
Ensure the associated script is designed to produce output when Hermes should deliver it, and no output when Hermes should remain silent, to leverage this mode effectively.
directives.target: hermes_config.yaml or cron_jobs.json (Hermes cron job configuration)
directives.rationale: This directive provides explicit steps to enable a new, cost-saving cron job execution mode by modifying configuration, making it highly implementable and testable.

directives.title: Utilize Hermes Video Analyze Tool
steps: Access the 'video analyze' tool within the Hermes system or an agent workflow.
Provide the video content (e.g., file path, URL, or data stream) as input to the tool.
Specify 'Gemini' or a 'compatible multimodal model' for performing the video analysis task.
directives.rationale: This directive specifies the use of a newly added, named tool within Hermes, including its inputs and model requirements, making it concrete for execution.

directives.title: Configure XAI Custom Voices as TTS Provider with Cloning
steps: Integrate 'XAI custom voices' as a new text-to-speech provider within Hermes system configuration.
Enable and configure 'voice cloning support' for the XAI TTS provider.
Select 'XAI custom voices' as the active text-to-speech provider for relevant voice workflows.
directives.rationale: This directive details the setup of a specific new TTS provider and its unique feature, clearly outlining configuration steps.

directives.title: Guide In-Flight Agent via /steer Command in IDE
steps: Ensure an agent is currently running an in-progress task within a supported IDE (Zed, VS Code, or JetBrains) with the ACP adapter activated.
Input the '/steer' command followed by specific guidance (e.g., '/steer prioritize fixing authentication flow') into the agent interaction interface.
directives.rationale: This provides explicit instructions for using a new, named interactive command within a well-defined context (IDE with ACP), making it highly actionable.

directives.title: Queue Follow-Up Work via /q Command in IDE
steps: Within a supported IDE (Zed, VS Code, or JetBrains) with the ACP adapter, initiate an agent interaction or observe an ongoing one.
Input the '/q' command followed by the description of the follow-up task (e.g., '/q next task: write unit tests for the new API endpoint') into the agent interaction interface.
directives.rationale: This directive clearly outlines how to use another new, named interactive command for scheduling tasks within specified IDEs, providing a concrete action.

directives.title: Enable and Use SearXNG Search Skill
steps: Access the Hermes skill management interface.
Locate and enable the 'SearXNG search' optional skill.
Configure any necessary backend connection details for the SearXNG instance within Hermes.
Integrate the SearXNG search skill into an agent's workflow for web research tasks.
directives.rationale: This directive provides steps to activate and utilize a specific new optional skill, including configuration and integration, making it implementable.

directives.title: Archive Curator Items using Subcommand
steps: Open the Hermes CLI or access the Curator tool interface.
Execute the command 'curator archive [item_id(s) / criteria]' to move specified items to the archive.
Optionally, verify the archival operation by listing archived items using 'curator list archive'.
directives.rationale: This directive provides explicit CLI command usage for a new Curator subcommand, detailing how to perform an archival action.

directives.title: Prune Curator Items using Subcommand
steps: Open the Hermes CLI or access the Curator tool interface.
Execute the command 'curator prune [item_id(s) / criteria]' to permanently remove specified items.
Verify the pruning by attempting to list or retrieve the pruned items, ensuring their removal.
directives.rationale: This directive provides explicit CLI command usage for a new Curator subcommand, detailing how to perform a pruning (deletion) action.

directives.title: List Archived Curator Items using Subcommand
steps: Open the Hermes CLI or access the Curator tool interface.
Execute the command 'curator list archive' to display all items currently residing in the archive.
directives.rationale: This directive provides explicit CLI command usage for a new Curator subcommand, detailing how to view the contents of the archive.
